chronic_inflammation.mechanism: Elevated CRP and WBC reflect systemic low-grade inflammation — 'inflammaging' — a core hallmark of biological aging associated with tissue damage, immune dysregulation, and accelerated mortality risk.

deregulated_nutrient_sensing.mechanism: Chronically elevated glucose signals insulin resistance and impaired nutrient-sensing pathways (mTOR, AMPK, insulin/IGF-1), a hallmark linked to accelerated cellular aging and metabolic disease.

loss_of_proteostasis.mechanism: Low serum albumin reflects impaired protein synthesis and reduced capacity to maintain protein homeostasis — a marker of hepatic function and systemic proteostatic stress.

mitochondrial_dysfunction.mechanism: Serum creatinine is a proxy for muscle mass and mitochondrial energy output. Declining creatinine in older adults often reflects sarcopenia and reduced mitochondrial capacity.

cellular_senescence.mechanism: Elevated RDW and MCV reflect hematopoietic stress and impaired red blood cell turnover — signatures of stem cell exhaustion and cellular senescence. Elevated ALP may reflect hepatic or bone senescent signaling.

altered_intercellular_communication.mechanism: Declining lymphocyte percentage reflects immunosenescence — the age-related deterioration of adaptive immunity and altered intercellular communication between immune cells.
